channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Follow NPR's annotated fact check of President Trump's address to Congress
item.description: As President Donald Trump addresses a joint session of Congress tonight, reporters from across NPR's newsroom will fact check and offer context as the speech unfolds.
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff, MPR News

item.title: ISIS-K member confessed to scouting Abbey Gate attack route, training Moscow attackers: affidavit
item.description: An ISIS-K member arrested following the 2021 Abbey Gate bombing in Afghanistan confessed to scouting the attack route, the Justice Department said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump pleads with people of Greenland to join the US
item.description: (The Hill) - President Trump on Tuesday said he’d welcome the people of Greenland to join the U.S. as he stressed that “I think we’re going to get” the self-governed Arctic territory within Denmark. "I also have a message tonight for the incredible people of Greenland.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: Divided Supreme Court rejects Trump administration's push to rebuke judge over foreign aid freeze
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A sharply divided Supreme Court on Wednesday rejected a Trump administration push to rebuke a federal judge who imposed a quick deadline to release billions of dollars in foreign aid. By a 5-4 vote, the court told U.S.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court denies Trump administration request to cancel $2 billion in foreign aid
item.description: The Supreme Court in a 5-4 emergency ruling Wednesday refused to halt a judge’s decision ordering the Trump administration to immediately release nearly $2 billion in foreign aid payments owed under existing contracts.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Suspect in Kabul airport bombing during Afghanistan pullout is caught and heading to US, Trump says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A suspected senior planner in the suicide bombing at the Kabul airport that killed 13 American service members during the chaotic withdrawal from Afghanistan has been taken into custody and will appear Wednesday in federal court in the United States to face charges.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER and ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The Latest: The world reacts to Trump's 'swift and unrelenting action' as president
item.description: President Donald Trump vowed more “swift and unrelenting action” reorienting the nation’s economy, immigration and foreign policy in an unyielding address before Congress as Democratic lawmakers showed their dissent.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: EXCLUSIVE: Elon Musk PAC thanks Trump for 'saving the American Dream' in new million-dollar ad
item.description: A PAC founded by billionaire X owner and Tesla CEO Elon Musk is going up on the national airwaves for the first time, thanking President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Spanish-language rebuttal to Trump’s joint address focuses on immigration
item.description: New York Rep. Adriano Espaillat delivered the Democrats’ Spanish-language rebuttal to President Trump’s joint address, focusing on the president’s immigration policy.
item.dc:creator: Ximena Bustillo, MPR News

item.title: Trump passes Clinton, gives longest modern address to joint session of Congress
item.description: President Donald Trump gave the longest address to a joint session of Congress in modern history on Tuesday night, surpassing former President Bill Clinton’s previous mark.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump says Zelenskyy wants peace, ready to accept minerals deal after WH blowup
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump said Tuesday that Ukrainian leader Volodymyr Zelenskyy had written to him to say he appreciates U.S. support for his country in its war with Russia and is ready to sign a deal that could ensure future American support.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI and JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Democrat Al Green removed from House after interrupting, heckling Trump
item.description: Rep. Al Green (D-Texas) was removed from the House chamber where President Trump was giving his address to a joint session of Congress after the congressman heckled the president early in his speech.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump vowed to end surprise medical bills. The team working on that just got slashed
item.description: Trump's first round of staff cuts to federal agencies eliminated dozens of positions at the Center for Consumer Information and Insurance Oversight, in charge of implementing the No Surprises Act.
item.dc:creator: Noam Levey, MPR News

item.title: Fired federal workers are among the guests invited to Trump's speech to Congress
item.description: The Trumps and lawmakers from across the aisle have invited guests to Tuesday's joint address, representing some of the administration's top priorities. Here's a look at some of the names on the list.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman, MPR News

item.title: Emboldened Trump praises his 'swift action' in address to Congress
item.description: President Donald Trump will stand before a joint session of Congress on Tuesday to give an accounting of his turbulent first weeks in office as a divided nation struggles to keep pace, with some Americans fearing for the country's future while others are cheering him on.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump threatens increased tariffs if Canada retaliates
item.description: President Donald Trump on Tuesday threatened to add additional taxes on Canada if the U.S.'s northern neighbor Canada retaliates with its own after Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau slammed Trump’s newly implemented tariffs.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Ukraine selling T-shirts with Zelensky quote from Trump-Vance meeting
item.description: The Ukrainian government has launched a T-shirt fundraising effort seeking to capitalize on Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky's now-infamous Oval Office meeting on Friday with President Trump and Vice President Vance.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, Fox 5

item.title: Democrats investigate DOJ for dropping charges on Mayor Adams
item.description: The lawmakers wrote a letter to Attorney General Pam Bondi on Sunday, accusing the DOJ of trying to cover up a quid pro quo.
item.dc:creator: Ailia Zehra, NewsNation

item.title: 46 percent say Trump changing economy for the worse: Survey
item.description: Recent polling has indicated that U.S. adults’ confidence in Trump’s ability to manage the country’s economy has been slipping.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Vance's comments about European peacekeeping plan for Ukraine draw ire abroad
item.description: Vice President Vance said the best security guarantee for Ukraine is to sign a mineral deal with the United States.
item.dc:creator: Franco Ordoñez, MPR News

item.title: A crucial, vacant Minnesota House seat will finally be filled next week
item.description: The March 11 special election will determine whether Republicans secure enough seats to pass bills in the Minnesota House or if they’ll fall back into a tie with the DFL.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Bondi says she was misled on Epstein documents
item.description: Attorney General Pam Bondi says she was misled into believing she had all the files related to the Epstein investigations, and is now seeking additional information from the FBI.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Mexico makes case to avoid U.S. tariffs as it awaits Trump’s decision
item.description: Mexico President Claudia Sheinbaum has said that her administration is waiting to see if U.S. President Donald Trump makes good on his threat to impose 25 percent tariffs on Mexican imports.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Judge extends block on Trump gender-affirming care federal funding ban
item.description: U.S. District Court Judge Lauren King ruled that President Trump’s executive orders violate the Constitution’s Fifth Amendment, which grants equal protection under the law.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, WFLA, NBC

item.title: California lawmaker revives AI whistleblower protections concerns in new bill
item.description: The California lawmaker who introduced an AI safety protection bill later vetoed by Gov. Gavin Newsom has again introduced legislation to regulate the powerful technology. Sen.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: Latex glove ban proposed in Nevada to help allergy sufferers
item.description: An allergy to latex can come with a lot of limits on where you can go and what you can do. Some clothing, medical procedures, and even visits to restaurants become potential problems.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Nevada film tax bill would bring jobs, but questions remain as lawmakers examine details
item.description: Lawmakers dug into details of the film tax credit bill on Thursday, with film studio executives from Warner Bros. Discovery and Sony answering questions in Carson City.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Nevada Gov. Lombardo, AG Ford clash on immigration policies ahead of potential 2026 matchup
item.description: Republican Nevada Gov. Joe Lombardo and Democratic Nevada Attorney General Aaron Ford -- who could face off in the 2026 gubernatorial election -- swapped jabs in competing statements regarding guidance about immigration enforcement.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News
